summary: App with YouTube integration and role-based access control, including banned/suspended user status screens.
architectural_notes: YouTube integration requires connection fix — has been persistently failing across multiple builds
known_issues: YouTube connection is not working despite previous fix attempts

existing_features.title: Remove any role-based access restrictions from YouTube-related features (channel connection, video URL input, YouTube player, clip suggestions, clip creation, post-to-YouTube). All authenticated users regardless of role (owner, admin, user, friend) must be able to see and use these features.

When an authenticated user's account status is 'banned' or 'suspended', display a full-screen error/blocking page instead of the normal app UI. The screen must clearly communicate that the account is banned or suspended and prevent access to all app features.

Fix the YouTube OAuth connection flow so that after a user completes the Google OAuth callback, the channel remains connected and does not revert to a disconnected state. Ensure the OAuth credentials are correctly stored in the backend, the actor is fully initialized before storing credentials, and all relevant React Query caches (channel connection status, OAuth credential status) are invalidated after a successful credential save so the UI reflects the connected state.

new_feature_requests.summary: All roles can access YouTube features
Show error screen to users with banned or suspended status
